hero.question: Will Anthropic have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
hero.prob_take: The -3.9 point drop signals serious doubt creeping in after Claude 3.5 Sonnet's underwhelming performance on recent benchmarks, with smart money finally pricing in that "best model" is a moving target that favors whoever ships the most capable reasoning or coding chops in Q1 2026. At 89.5% you're still massively overweighting Anthropic's brand inertia over the brutal reality that OpenAI, Google, and Xai are all shipping hard right now, and "best" gets defined by whoever owns the benchmark crown in March.

daily_take.headline: Anthropic's dominance bet just got cheaper. Smart money moving in.
daily_take.deck: Anthropic's odds to lead AI by March 2026 dropped 3.9 points to 89.5% on $866K volume. The move looks like profit-taking or model release anxiety rather than real fundamental shift. At this price, you're getting a discount on a company that's still releasing stronger models than competitors. This is a buy-the-dip situation for anyone with conviction in Claude's trajectory.
